research_questions: Do users actually want current-window export, or are they asking for cross-window or session backup?
Is the strongest pain about CSV cleanliness, session restore, sharing lists, backup, or support handoff?
Can the evidence support a much narrower single-purpose wedge instead of generic tab export?
Can the wedge be differentiated from the existing tab_csv_window_export portfolio?
Is there a deterministic happy path we can test without flaky external dependencies?
Would the user install a dedicated extension for this narrower export job?
Can the wedge stay within low-risk tabs, downloads, and storage permissions?
Is there a stronger alternative wedge elsewhere in discovery that should replace this candidate instead?

targeted_evidence_plan.query: current window only export complaints and scope confusion
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: Repeated statements that whole-session export is too broad.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Use existing support tickets and store reviews as bounded evidence only.

targeted_evidence_plan.query: CSV cleanup and noisy column complaints
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: Users want a clean, share-ready export rather than a raw backup dump.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Keep the output-format wedge in still_research_more.

targeted_evidence_plan.query: pinned tabs, filename, and export-scope complaints
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: Secondary requests that can confirm or weaken the narrow export hypothesis.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Do not treat store-only evidence as sufficient differentiation.

targeted_evidence_plan.query: support handoff or researcher workflow requiring tab snapshot export
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: Independent corroboration for a dedicated install-worthy job.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Keep confidence capped and avoid upgrading a same-family build.

targeted_evidence_plan.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com current window tab export csv pinned tabs review
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: All-session export is too broad and noisy.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Record live_unavailable and keep the candidate in research_more or skip.

targeted_evidence_plan.query: site:github.com browser tab export extension issue current window csv
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: Noisy export columns and weak success feedback.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Record live_unavailable and keep the candidate in research_more or skip.

targeted_evidence_plan.query: site:github.com browser extension one-click data cleanup issue
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: Too many cleanup steps before data is shareable.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Record live_unavailable and keep the candidate in research_more or skip.

targeted_evidence_plan.query: site:chromewebstore.google.com browser workflow friction local-only productivity review
targeted_evidence_plan.expected_signal: Heavy dashboards for tasks that should be single-click.
targeted_evidence_plan.fallback_if_unavailable: Record live_unavailable and keep the candidate in research_more or skip.

additional_evidence_collected.summary: The strongest repeated signal is about narrowing export scope to the current window instead of the full session.
Users complain about CSV noise because they want a share-ready handoff artifact, not a raw backup dump.
Pinned-tab and filename requests look like secondary refinements, not the primary install trigger.
Permission trust and clear success feedback matter, but they look more like acceptance requirements than the wedge itself.

unresolved_uncertainties: No Reddit or practitioner forum evidence yet.
Discovery still depends on fixture-mode evidence, so freshness is capped.
At least one top pain cluster is still weak or overly generic.
Discovery is still fixture-backed, so live corroboration is capped.

wedge_hypotheses.one_sentence_value: Export only the current Chrome window to a clean, share-ready CSV in one click.
wedge_hypotheses.target_user: Researchers, coordinators, and operators handing off a focused tab set.
wedge_hypotheses.trigger_moment: When a user needs to share or archive only the tabs visible in the current window.
pain_addressed: Full-session export is broader than the job to be done.
Current CSV output is noisy and requires manual cleanup before sharing.
Users do not trust exporters that appear to read every tab by default.
wedge_hypotheses.happy_path_test: Open two browser windows, export from the active one, verify only current-window rows and a clean CSV column set.
wedge_hypotheses.why_this_is_not_a_clone: The scope is narrower and the output is cleaner, but the trigger moment and artifact are still very close to the existing QuickTab CSV portfolio.

wedge_hypotheses.one_sentence_value: Export only pinned tabs from the current window to a compact CSV for repeated research or ops handoff.
wedge_hypotheses.target_user: Users who curate a pinned working set and repeatedly share that subset.
wedge_hypotheses.trigger_moment: When only pinned tabs represent the durable working set worth exporting.
pain_addressed: Users want a smaller working-set export.
They want filename control and less post-export cleanup.
wedge_hypotheses.happy_path_test: Pin a subset of tabs, export only pinned tabs from the current window, verify unpinned rows are excluded.
wedge_hypotheses.why_this_is_not_a_clone: The output filter is narrower, but the evidence is still too thin to justify a separate install-worthy wedge.

wedge_hypotheses.one_sentence_value: Create a privacy-bounded current-window export with explicit success feedback so users know the snapshot actually downloaded.
wedge_hypotheses.target_user: Support and operations users who need a dependable handoff snapshot without ambiguity.
wedge_hypotheses.trigger_moment: When a user must prove the current-window snapshot was captured and downloaded successfully.
pain_addressed: Silent export failures make the tool feel broken.
Users want confidence that only the intended window was touched.
wedge_hypotheses.happy_path_test: Export current window and verify visible success feedback plus downloaded file presence.
wedge_hypotheses.why_this_is_not_a_clone: The trust angle is somewhat sharper, but demand is not yet strong enough to prove a dedicated wedge.

selected_wedge_hypothesis.one_sentence_value: Export only the current Chrome window to a clean, share-ready CSV in one click.
selected_wedge_hypothesis.target_user: Researchers, coordinators, and operators handing off a focused tab set.
selected_wedge_hypothesis.trigger_moment: When a user needs to share or archive only the tabs visible in the current window.
pain_addressed: Full-session export is broader than the job to be done.
Current CSV output is noisy and requires manual cleanup before sharing.
Users do not trust exporters that appear to read every tab by default.
selected_wedge_hypothesis.happy_path_test: Open two browser windows, export from the active one, verify only current-window rows and a clean CSV column set.
selected_wedge_hypothesis.why_this_is_not_a_clone: The scope is narrower and the output is cleaner, but the trigger moment and artifact are still very close to the existing QuickTab CSV portfolio.

updated_pain_clusters.specific_user_action: export only the tabs in the active browser window
download a compact CSV that needs no manual cleanup before sharing
confirm that the current-window snapshot downloaded successfully
updated_pain_clusters.weak_cluster_reason: Only one recent source mentions silent export failure or missing confirmation.

updated_pain_clusters.specific_user_action: trust that export touches only the intended current window
updated_pain_clusters.weak_cluster_reason: Permission-trust positioning still depends on a single strong complaint.

updated_score_breakdown.selected_wedge_hypothesis: Export only the current Chrome window to a clean, share-ready CSV in one click.
decision_rationale: The research loop sharpened the wedge from generic tab export into a specific current-window CSV job.
Evidence quality and testability stayed strong, and wedge clarity improved materially.
That sharper wedge now overlaps too directly with the existing tab_csv_window_export portfolio, so the candidate should not build.

product_acceptance_forecast_gate.reason: The narrowed wedge now maps to a deterministic, low-permission happy path.

final_decision_rationale: The research loop sharpened the wedge from generic tab export into a specific current-window CSV job.
Evidence quality and testability stayed strong, and wedge clarity improved materially.
That sharper wedge now overlaps too directly with the existing tab_csv_window_export portfolio, so the candidate should not build.
The candidate became clear enough to evaluate, and that clarity shows it should be skipped because it is too close to the existing portfolio.
